title: National Biobank and Phenome India Project
focus: Details of the National Biobank, PI-Check cohort, and the definition of phenome/phenotype.
rationale: This section covers a specific CSIR initiative (PI-Check) and the associated National Biobank, along with the foundational biological concept of a phenome.
sectionExcerpt: The National Biobank, launched under the Phenome India Project (PI-Check) by CSIR, is based on the UK Biobank model customized for Indian diversity. It aims to collect genomic, lifestyle, and clinical data from 10,000 individuals to aid early diagnosis and AI-driven diagnostics. A phenome is the entire set of phenotypes (observable physical characteristics determined by genotype and environment) in an organism.
evidenceBullets: Launched under Phenome India Project (PI-Check) by CSIR.
Based on the UK Biobank model, customized for Indian diversity.
Collects genomic, lifestyle, and clinical data from 10,000 individuals.
Phenome is the entire set of phenotypes in a cell, tissue, organ, organism or species.
Phenotype is determined by genotype and environmental influences.

title: Biochemical Markers
focus: Definition and application of biochemical markers, specifically for detecting kidney complications in diabetic patients.
rationale: A distinct, small topic focusing on a specific medical discovery regarding biochemical markers for kidney complications.
sectionExcerpt: Indian researchers identified biochemical markers in blood to detect kidney complications in diabetic patients. Biochemical markers are small molecules (sugars, amino acids, lipids) produced during metabolic processes used to ascertain disease risks. Components for identifying kidney complications include arabitol, myo-inositol, and ribothymidine.
evidenceBullets: Biochemical markers are small molecules produced during metabolic processes used to ascertain disease risks.
Examples include sugars, amino acids, and lipids.
Arabitol, myo-inositol, and ribothymidine are components used for identifying kidney complications in diabetic patients.

title: National Biofoundry Network and BioE3 Policy
focus: India's first National Biofoundry Network and the BioE3 Policy launched by DBT.
rationale: Covers India's biotechnology policy framework (BioE3) and its infrastructure component (Biofoundry Network).
sectionExcerpt: India's first National Biofoundry Network was launched as a Bio enabler component of the BioE3 Policy to scale up proof-of-concept projects. A Biofoundry is an automated facility integrating DNA synthesis, gene editing, and biomanufacturing. The BioE3 (Biotechnology for Economy, Environment and Employment) Policy, launched by DBT under the Ministry of Science & Technology, aims to revolutionize biomanufacturing in sectors like precision biotherapeutics and smart proteins.
evidenceBullets: National Biofoundry Network is a component of the BioE3 Policy, categorized as Bio enablers.
Biofoundry is an automated facility integrating DNA synthesis, gene editing, and biomanufacturing.
BioE3 (Biotechnology for Economy, Environment and Employment) Policy is India's first policy in Biotechnology.
BioE3 was launched by the DBT, under the Ministry of Science & Technology.
BioE3 aims to revolutionize biomanufacturing, including precision biotherapeutics and carbon capture.

title: DEEP Project and Epigenetics
focus: The Diverse Epigenetic Epidemiology Partnership (DEEP) project and the concept of epigenetics.
rationale: Focuses on a specific international collaboration involving CSIR-CCMB and the scientific concept of epigenetics.
sectionExcerpt: CSIR-CCMB is collaborating on the Diverse Epigenetic Epidemiology Partnership (DEEP) project, a five-year initiative led by the University of Bristol and CSIR-CCMB to explore genomic and environmental diversity effects on disease risk. Epigenetics studies how behaviors and environment cause reversible changes that affect gene function without altering the DNA sequence.
evidenceBullets: DEEP is a five-year project led by the University of Bristol and CSIR-CCMB in India.
DEEP aims to explore the effects of genomic and environmental diversity on disease risk.
Epigenetics studies how behaviors and environment cause changes affecting gene function.
Epigenetic changes are reversible and do not change the DNA sequence, but alter how the body reads it.
